witnesses.description: A three-point finite topology whose specialization preorder is a chain c <= b <= a.
An indiscrete two-point topology whose specialization preorder is not antisymmetric.

checks.claim: The listed finite topology induces the stated specialization preorder.
checks.notes: The validator recomputes x <= y by checking that every open set containing x also contains y.

checks.claim: The specialization relation agrees with singleton closures: x <= y iff x is in closure({y}).
checks.notes: The validator recomputes each singleton closure from finite open-set complements and compares it against the specialization pairs.

checks.claim: The finite topology is T0, so its specialization preorder is antisymmetric.
checks.notes: The validator checks that no two distinct points mutually specialize, so the finite preorder is a partial order.

checks.claim: The indiscrete two-point topology is T0 and has an antisymmetric specialization preorder.

data.certificate: prove_qf_uf_unsat_alethe emits Evidence::UnsatAletheProof; Evidence::check independently rechecks the proof against the source assertions.

checks.notes: Finite replay computes mutual specialization for x and y. The false T0/antisymmetry claim would force x = y, contradicting the distinct-point assertion in the checked QF_UF/Alethe artifact.

checks.claim: General specialization-order theory requires future proof resources.

data.target_theorem_shape: T0 quotients, Kolmogorov separation, sobriety, Alexandroff spaces, continuity as monotone maps for specialization preorders, and domain-theoretic order for arbitrary spaces
data.future_checker: Lean modules for topology, separation axioms, order theory, quotient spaces, sober spaces, and proof reconstruction from finite specialization certificates

checks.notes: The finite rows replay one explicit space and one false T0 claim; they do not prove arbitrary-space specialization-order theorems.
